reasoning: Confirmed open redirect at line 409 of class-forceregeneratethumbnails.php. The $_GET['goback'] parameter is echoed as an href value after passing through esc_url() and sanitize_text_field(), neither of which restricts the destination to the same host. esc_url() strips javascript:/vbscript:/data: schemes but allows arbitrary https:// URLs including https://evil.com. There is no wp_validate_redirect(), home_url() check, or allowed-hosts allowlist anywhere in the file. The goback rendering at line 408-409 is outside the nonce-gated processing block (that check at line 431 only guards the regeneration logic); the link itself renders whenever goback is non-empty and the user is authenticated to the admin page. Reachability requires the victim be authenticated with manage_options (default capability), navigate to a crafted URL, complete a thumbnail regeneration (requires a valid nonce which the attacker cannot forge without a separate CSRF vector), and then click the poisoned 'Go back' link in the success banner. Multiple steps and admin-level authentication make practical exploitation difficult, justifying the low severity rating. The finding accurately describes the code path and the limitation of esc_url() as a domain filter.
